# Facebook Post — Enterprise AI Adoption Hits 53% Globally — Agentic AI Becomes Core Infrastructure
# Week: Week of 2026-04-20
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-04-26T23:02:42.338Z

---

Agentic AI just stopped being optional.

Stanford's 2026 AI Index confirms what enterprise leaders have been sensing: generative AI has hit 53% global adoption in three years, outpacing both the PC and the internet at the same stage. 70% of companies now deploy it in at least one function.

But the real signal from April 2026 is not about adoption numbers. It is about infrastructure. Salesforce, ServiceNow, Oracle, EY, and Microsoft all shipped production-grade autonomous agent platforms in the same month. Salesforce built dedicated hubs on Agentforce. ServiceNow expanded Now Assist into a full orchestration layer. These companies are not experimenting anymore — they are building the rails.

Here is the executive problem: Infor surveyed 1,000 business decision-makers across the US, UK, Germany, and France. More than half still struggle to scale AI beyond initial deployments. The technology is ready. The organizational architecture often is not.

The competitive window is narrowing. Companies that treat agentic AI as a systems architecture challenge — not a feature to evaluate next quarter — will lock in structural advantages in cost, speed, and operational resilience.

GYanTech AI helps leadership teams design and deploy agentic AI systems that actually scale. Start the conversation at www.gyantechai.com

## Fact-check notes
- Stanford 2026 AI Index reports generative AI at 53% global population adoption three years in, with 70% of companies deploying it in at least one function (Source: Stanford AI Index 2026 via Asanify digest)
- Salesforce and ServiceNow both launched dedicated autonomous agent hubs in April 2026, with Salesforce building on Agentforce and ServiceNow expanding Now Assist into a full agent orchestration layer (Source: Auton AI News, April 20 2026)
- EY, Oracle, Salesforce, Hyland, and Microsoft all shipped production-grade agentic AI deployments in April 2026, coordinating multi-agent workflows across audit, engineering, manufacturing, and operations (Source: Fifth Row blog, April 2026)
- Infor Enterprise AI Adoption Impact Index surveyed 1,000 business decision-makers across the US, UK, Germany, and France, finding more than half of businesses struggle to scale AI (Source: Infor press release, April 22 2026)
